mission.title: Install and Configure Container Network Interface Cni- on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Container Network Interface (CNI) provides a specification and libraries for writing plugins to configure network interfaces in Linux containers. Installing CNI allows you to manage network connectivity for your containerized applications efficiently.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for CNI
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for the CNI installation to keep resources organized.

steps.title: Download and Apply CNI Manifests
steps.description: Apply the CNI manifests from the GitHub repository to set up the necessary components.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the CNI components are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of CNI will show the CNI pods running in your specified namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC permissions for the CNI deployment.

uninstall.title: Remove CNI Resources
uninstall.description: Delete the CNI resources using the following command.

upgrade.title: Upgrade CNI to a New Version
upgrade.description: To upgrade CNI, download the new version of the manifests and apply them.

troubleshooting.title: CNI Pods Not Running
troubleshooting.description: If the CNI pods are not running, check the logs of the pods for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n cni`.

troubleshooting.title: Network Connectivity Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you experience network connectivity issues, ensure that the CNI configuration files are correctly applied and that the CNI plugins are installed properly.

troubleshooting.title: Version Compatibility
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the CNI version is compatible with your Kubernetes version. Check the CNI documentation for supported versions.

prerequisites.description: Ensure that you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your machine.
